content.problemStatement:
In this example, we demonstrate how to use Python’s `join()` method to insert a hyphen (“-”) between each character in the string “PYTHON,” creating a stylized version of the word.

Consider the following variable:

`word = "PYTHON"
`When the given code is executed, the output will be:

`P-Y-T-H-O-N
`
constraints: S contains only uppercase English letters
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases (1 ≤ T ≤ 100). Each of the next T lines contains a non‑empty string S consisting of uppercase English letters (1 ≤ |S| ≤ 100).
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line with the characters of S separated by a hyphen ('-').

sampleTestCases.explanation: There is one test case. The word PYTHON is transformed by inserting a hyphen between every two consecutive characters.
First test case: HELLO becomes H-E-L-L-O. Second test case: a single character A remains unchanged because there is no other character to separate with a hyphen.
Each word is processed independently, inserting hyphens between its letters. The single‑letter word C stays as C.

solution.approach: For each test case read the string S. The required output is simply the characters of S joined by the hyphen character. This can be done by iterating over the characters and printing a hyphen after every character except the last one, or by using language‑specific join utilities (e.g., '-'.join(s) in Python, s.split('').join('-') in JavaScript). The algorithm runs in linear time with respect to the total length of all strings and uses only O(1) additional memory per test case (apart from the output buffer).
solution.timeComplexity: O(total length of all input strings) – each character is processed once.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) auxiliary space (output is written directly).
